posts.text: Dev thriends: what are your thoughts on Calendar Versioning (CalVer) vs the traditional Semantic Versioning (SemVer)? I ask because I'm leaning towards switching Rackula to CalVer, specifically YY. MM. MICRO format. My reasoning is it removes some of the annoying friction I'm finding around deciding what is a minor vs major release on SemVer style releasing. The other thought I have is that because Rackula isn't a library, the actual version number is less important than release date freshness

Released Rackula v0.10.1 with a few bug fixes and adjustments. Web on Mobile with SVGs is hard! ...anyways, you can click on things now, so that's nice. 

https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v0.10.1

Just released Rackula v0.10.0 with a thwack of security and bug fixes, plus two new features:
- support for AutoCAD import via refined vector PDF export
- drag and drop between racks (how did I not have this?!) 

https://count.racku.la/
Changelog: https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v0.10.0

Introducing nfs-swiftbar: a plugin for SwiftBar that shows the status of NFS (Network File System) mounts in macOS. 

I basically got tired of having to open up a terminal every time my NFS shares acted up, so I threw together (using Claude) a handy little tool to smack 'em until they work. 

For the seven people that use macOS and NFS together, you're welcome. 

https://github.com/ggfevans/nfs-swiftbar
